LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED; 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. AS TO THE FRAUD CLAIM, THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THE LANS AND IT LAWYERS ACTED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF. THIS ALLEGATION IS SUFFICIENT TO ALLEGE A FIDUCIARY DUTY. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT AS TO THE IMMUNITY OF THE REGENTS DOES NOT DISPOSE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH ALLEGES WRONGFUL CONDUCT ON THE PART OF LANS AFTER THE ASSIGNMENT IN PARAGRAPHS 33 AND 34. THE EQUAL ACCESS ARGUMENT IS A FACTUAL, NOT A PLEADING ISSUE. THE ABSENCE OF A COPY OF THE LICENSE AGREEMENT PRECLUDES THE COURT FROM SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER BASED ON THE CONTENTION THAT ONLY A BREACH OF CONTRACT IS BEING ALLEGED. THE DEMURRER TO THE IMPLIED COVENANT ALSO CANNOT BE ADDRESSED ON DEMURRER WHERE THE CONTRACT IS NOT A PART OF THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. THE COURT NOTED THIS CASE WOULD BENEFIT FROM A SINGLE ASSIGNMENT TO A JUDICIAL OFFICER. IF COUNSEL AGREE ON SINGLE ASSIGNMENT, THEY ARE TO CALL THE CLERK FOR DEPARTMENT 302 AND ADVISE THE COURT OF THE AGREED UPON JUDGE OR A SHORT LIST OF JUDICIAL OFFICERS. AN ORDER FOR SINGLE ASSIGNMENT WOULD THEN BE GENERATED FROM THE PRESIDING JUDGE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT CALDERA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.'S DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, LLC. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE DEMURRER TO LANS' CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, FIRST COUNT, FOR LANS NEED NOT ALLEGE COMPLIANCE WITH THE NOTICE/DEFAULT PROVISION FOR THE REASONS STATED IN CONNECTION WITH THE DEMURRER TO THE REGENTS' CROSS-COMPLAINT; CAUSATION AND DAMAGES ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, SECOND COUNT AND THIRD COUNT, WHICH MUST BE PLEAD, AS A MATTER OF FORM, AS CAUSES OF ACTION, RATHER THAN COUNTS, AS AN ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF LIABILITY. WILLIAMS V. MARSHALL (1951) 37 CAL.2D 445, 457. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LANS TO PLEAD, AS DISTINCT CAUSES OF ACTION, BREACH OF THE OPTION AGREEMENT AND THE LICENSE AGREEMENT. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INDUCING BREACH OF CONTRACT. PARAGRAPHS 12-17 PLEAD THE REQUISITE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. THERE ARE FACTUAL ISSUES AS TO WHAT CAPACITY DR. WARNER WAS ACTING IN AT THE TIME OF THE ALLEGED BREACH THEREBY PRECLUDING SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER. WOODS V. FOX BROADCASTING SUB. INC. (2005) !29 CAL.APP.4TH 344, 355-356. DAMAGES ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. THE DEMURRER TO THE TORT CLAIMS IS OVERRULED FOR THE REASONS STATED IN CONNECTION WITH THE CALDERA DEMURRER. Off Calendar Jan-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT CALDERA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.'S DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA.
APPEARANCES: Charles J. Stiegler, Esq. of Law Offices of Gorman & Miller 408/297-2222 for pltf/x-deft & moving party Caldera; Charlene M. Morrow, Esq. of Fenwick & West LLP 650/335-7826 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties The Regents of the University of Califorina and Los Alamos National Security, L.L.C.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Caldera's demurrer to the Regents' cross-complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part with 10 days leave to amend. It is sustained as to the first cause of action for the Regents have alleged a breach of two contracts and must plead the breach of each contract in a separate cause of action. The Regents need not allege compliance with the notice/default provision of the license agreement for it is, at most, a condition of termination of the contract, not a condition to a suit for breach. Moreover, the language is permissive. Causation and damages are adequately alleged. The demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action based on Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97 and the third cause of action based on Thrifty-Tel, Inc. v. Bezenek (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1559. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

APPEARANCES: Charles J. Stiegler, Esq. of Law Offices of Gorman & Miller 408/297-2222 for pltf/x-deft & moving party Caldera; Charlene M. Morrow, Esq. of Fenwick & West LLP 650/335-7826 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties The Regents of the University of Califorina and Los Alamos National Security, L.L.C.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - The demurrer to LANS' cross-complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part with 10 days leave to amend. It is overruled as to the first cause of action, first count, for LANS need not allege compliance with the notice/default provision for the reasons stated in connection with the demurrer to the Regents' cross-complaint; causation and damages are adequately alleged. The demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action, second count and third count, which must be plead, as a matter of form, as causes of action, rather than counts, as an alternative theories of liability. Williams v. Marshall (1951) 37 Cal.2d 445, 457. The demurrer is sustained as to the second cause of action for LANS to plead, as distinct causes of action, breach of the option agreement and the license agreement. The demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action for inducing breach of contract. Paragraphs 12-17 plead the requisite terms of the contract. There are factual issues as to what capacity Dr. Warner was acting in at the time of the alleged breach thereby precluding sustaining the demurrer. Woods v. Fox Broadcasting Sub. Inc. (2005) !29 Cal.App.4th 344, 355-356. Damages are adequately alleged. The demurrer to the tort claims is overruled for the reasons stated in connection with the Caldera demurrer. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Gorman of Gorman & Miller (408.297.2222) for plaintiff and responding party
Charlene Morrow of Fenwick & West (650.335.7155) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled; 10 days to answer. As to the fraud claim, the amended complaint alleges the LANS and it lawyers acted as counsel for plaintiff. This allegation is sufficient to allege a fiduciary duty. Defendant's argument as to the immunity of the Regents does not dispose of the cause of action which alleges wrongful conduct on the part of LANS after the assignment in paragraphs 33 and 34. The equal access argument is a factual, not a pleading issue. The absence of a copy of the license agreement precludes the Court from sustaining the demurrer based on the contention that only a breach of contract is being alleged. The demurrer to the implied covenant also cannot be addressed on demurrer where the contract is not a part of the record. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. The Court noted this case would benefit from a single assignment to a judicial officer. If counsel agree on single assignment, they are to call the clerk for Department 302 and advise the Court of the agreed upon Judge or a short list of judicial officers. An order for single assignment would then be generated from the Presiding Judge. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT:
APPEARANCES: JOHN C. GORMAN, ESQUIRE (GORMAN & MILLER, 408.297.2222) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. CHARLENE M. MORROW, ESQUIRE AND ERIN C. JONES, ESQUIRE (FENWICK & WEST, 650.335.7155) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING: The demurrer of LANS to the second cause of action is sustained WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. The amended complaint alleges that patent application '376 had not been timely filed prior to the execution of the License Agreement at issue (paragraphs 29-30) and that plaintiff was not aware of the fact that patent application '901 had been abandoned prior to the execution of the License Agreement (paragraphs 13 and 32). Plaintiff alleges that it incurred substantial costs based on the belief that these patents were viable and defendants failed to disclose the fact that the rights to '376 and '901 had not been perfected. From the face of the complaint, the wrongs about which plaintiff complains occurred prior to the assignment to LANS on April 25, 2006 and plaintiff had already sustained an injury as a consequence. Based on these facts, LANS, as assignee of a public entity, is entitled to immunity for the conduct of the Regents in abandoning '901 and failing to make the requisite filings as to the '376 as well as the alleged breach of duty on the part of the Regents in not disclosing these facts. Merely because LANS became an assignee does not give rise to a duty distinct from the duty to disclose to plaintiff that the Regents already allegedly breached. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
